title: ‘SNL’ recap: Best moments from Jack Black hosting ‘Saturday Night Live’
author: Kevin Slane
bullets: Jack Black hosted 'SNL' for the fifth time on April 4, marking his entry into the five-timers club.
Jack White performed as a musical guest, playing an SNL-themed version of 'Seven Nation Army'.
The episode featured appearances by notable five-timers Jonah Hill, Tina Fey, Candice Bergen, and Melissa McCarthy.

title: Jaylen Brown opens up about past criticisms of his left hand: ‘There was some truth to it’
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Jaylen Brown acknowledges past struggles with his left hand due to a major wrist injury and re-injuries.
Brown's improvements with his left hand contribute to a career-high 28.7 points per game this season.
Brown attributes recent success to recovery efforts, including stem cell treatments, aiding flexibility and motion.

title: ‘The Red Sox were my dream job’: Don Orsillo reflects on NESN tenure, reunites with former colleagues
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Don Orsillo returned to Boston with the San Diego Padres after nearly 11 years since leaving NESN.
Orsillo cherished his NESN tenure and expressed gratitude for his ongoing role with the Padres alongside partner Mark Grant.
Former NESN colleagues, including Jim Rice, warmly reunited with Orsillo during his visit, highlighting enduring respect and fond memories.

title: Alex Cora explains his decision to slot Roman Anthony at DH early this season
author: Colin McCarthy
bullets: Roman Anthony has struggled defensively, prompting Red Sox manager Alex Cora to use him as designated hitter.
Anthony contributed offensively against the Padres, hitting a triple and aiding a runner to third base.
Boston Red Sox holds a 2-6 record as they face the Padres for the series' final game at Fenway Park.

title: Connelly Early gave Red Sox a chance to win despite high pitch count, lack of command
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox pitcher Connelly Early allowed three hits and four walks in four innings, struggling with control against the Padres.
Despite Early's effort, the Red Sox lost 3-2 to the Padres, with Aroldis Chapman giving up the winning run in the ninth.
Early's performance featured his 39th career strikeout, tying Red Sox history with Daisuke Matsuzaka for most in first six games.

title: 4 takeaways from the Revolution’s 3-0 win over CF Montreal
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: The New England Revolution defeated CF Montreal 3-0 in their second home game, improving their 2026 season record.
Revolution head coach Marko Mitrović was red-carded in the 34th minute for leaving the technical area during the game.
Revolution players Luca Langoni, Mamadou Fofana, and Peyton Miller each scored, with Langoni also providing an assist.

title: Here’s the latest on Red Sox’ Triston Casas, Johan Oviedo injuries
author: Kaley Brown
bullets: Red Sox's Triston Casas will begin hitting from a tee on Monday after rib injury diagnosis as intercostal strain.
Johan Oviedo to meet with Tommy John surgeon after right elbow strain and velocity drop in recent outing.
Kutter Crawford and Patrick Sandoval nearing minor league rehab outings after long injury recoveries.

title: This 22-year-old cancer survivor is running Boston for MGH
author: Kelly Chan
bullets: Grace Taylor, a 22-year-old cancer survivor from Dedham, is running the Boston Marathon for Mass General Hospital.
Taylor was diagnosed with aggressive thyroid cancer but received specialized treatment at Mass General, leading to her recovery.
The marathon is a tribute to her doctors, the AYA program, and fellow cancer patients and survivors.

title: South Boston runner supports BMC social workers in Boston Marathon
author: Autumn Sloboda
bullets: Paige Beattie, 23, from South Boston, is running the 2026 Boston Marathon with Team BMC to support social workers.
Beattie aims to raise $10,000 for Boston Medical Center's safety-net mission and its social worker programs.
Fundraising efforts include HandleBar classes and Stats Bar events; funds enhance BMC's patient care services.

title: Patience remains an elusive virtue for Legacy FC attack
author: Hayden Bird
bullets: Boston Legacy FC lost 1-0 to San Diego Wave at Gillette Stadium, continuing their search for a first NWSL win.
Coach Filipa Patão emphasized patience, acknowledging the team's rushed play in the final third against San Diego.
Forward Amanda Gutierres struggled to connect, completing 11 of 21 passes, and has yet to score this season.
